title: ContactProbe Properties
description:
ContactProbe Properties

ContactProbe Properties

The following list is a summary of properties for the ContactProbe object.  The details for each property are explained in the Description field.

Property	Description

Name Property	This property can set a particular name that is assigned to objects. When ContactProbe object is created, name is assigned automatically.  For the assigned name, number is added after ContactProbe such as ContactProbe01.

You can change the name and up to 32 characters can be specified.  English one byte characters and underscore [ _ ] are available.  Also, you cannot use number for initial character.

Description Property	This property can set explanations for objects.  The explanation can be freely written using up to 255 characters.

Enabled Property	This property can set whether to enable the object.  When specifying True, the program executes the object.  When specifying False, the program executes the next object without executing the object.  Use this property when you want to save the sequence temporary without executing it or try with different parameters by copying objects during sequence creation.

AutoStepID Property	This property can set an automatic assignment of StepID in object execution.  When specifying True, assign StepID automatically.  When specifying False, it will be the value specified by StepID Property.  When assigning automatically, (sequence number *100 + object number) is set.  StepID is recorded to files that the force or the position during the sequence execution is recorded.  It is used to determine which interval corresponds to which object.

StepID Property	This property can set StepID during the object execution.  It is only used when AutoStepID is False.

AbortSeqOnFail Property	This property can set operations when the object fails.  When specifying True, the program ends the sequence and proceeds to the next SPEL statement if the object fails.  When specifying False, the program proceeds to the next force guide object without ending the sequence even if the object fails.  It is used when you want to continue the sequence (ex: the recovery processes are included in the sequence when the object fails.)

IOPreprocessEnabled Property	This property can set I/O operations when the object starts.  When specifying True, the program executes I/O operation.  When specifying False, the program does not execute I/O operation.  I/O operations are defined by IOPreprocOutputBit Property and IOPreprocOutputStatus Property.  It is used when you want to operate the hands or peripherals before the object execution.

IOPreprocessOutputBit Property	This property can set I/O operations (output bit) when the object starts.  It is only used when IOPreprocEnabled is True.

IOPreprocessOutputStatus Property	This property can set output status in I/O operations when the object starts.  When specifying On, set the specified output bit to On (set to 1).  When specifying Off, set the specified output bit to Off (set to 0).  It is only used when IOPreprocEnabled is True.

ProbeTrajectory Property	This property can set trajectory of the object.  When specifying Straight, repeat the contacting motion by moving the start position along with the straight line.  When specifying Spiral, repeat the contacting motion by moving the start position along with the spiral.

AccelS Property	This property can set the acceleration when moving to the start position of the contact.

SpeedS Property	This property can set the speed when moving to the start position of the contact.

SpiralDiam Property	This property can set the diameter of the spiral trajectory.  It is used when specifying Spiral in ProbeTrajectory property.

SpiralPitch Property	This property can set the pitch of the spiral trajectory.  It is used when specifying Spiral in ProbeTrajectory property.

DestRelativeX Property	For the straight line trajectory, this property can set the moving amount in X direction of coordinate systems specified by ForceOrient and RobotLocal of the sequence.  It is used when specifying Straight in ProbeTrajectory property.

DestRelativeY Property	For the straight line trajectory, this property can set the moving amount in Y direction of coordinate systems specified by ForceOrient and RobotLocal of the sequence.  It is used when specifying Straight in ProbeTrajectory property.

DestRelativeZ Property	For the straight line trajectory, this property can set the moving amount in Z direction of coordinate systems specified by ForceOrient and RobotLocal of the sequence.  It is used when specifying Straight in ProbeTrajectory property.

ContactInterval Property	This property can set the moving amount of the contact start position.  When contacting during the contacting motion, the amount specified by ContactInterval and the position moved by the specified trajectory will be the start position of the next contacting motion.

ContactOrient Property	This property can specify the direction of a hole.  You can set the direction in the coordinate system specified by ForceOrient of the sequence.  You can select from translation direction (+Fx ~ -Fz).  The robot moves to the specified direction and searches the hole.

ContactDist Property	This property can set an expected distance between the start position and the hole position.  When moving for ContactDist + ContactDistMargin, the program determines that the hole is detected and proceeds to the next force guide object.

ContactDistMargin Property	This property can set a margin that is added to the distance between the start position and the hole position.  Make sure to set a value with consideration of the maximum difference.  When moving for ContactDist + ContactDistMargin, the program determines that the hole is detected and proceeds to the next force guide object.

CFEnabled Property	This property set whether to continue the force control functions after the objects are ended.  When specifying True, the force control functions will continue even if the objects are ended.  When specifying False, the force control functions will end when the objects are ended.  When the sequence ends even if CFEnabled is True, the force control functions will end.

ContactForceThresh Property	This property can set threshold of force to determine contact.  When the value specified by this property is exceeded during the execution of Contact object, the robot returns to the start position of the contacting motion and moves to the next start position.

PlaneNumber Property	This property can set the Plane number to be used of the end conditions related to positions.  When specifying Plane in the PlaneType property, Plane of the specified number will not be changed.  When specifying Relative in the PlaneType property, redefine a new Plane to the specified number each time the object is executed.  Therefore, note that the original setting will be lost.

PlaneEndCond Property	For the end conditions related to positions, this property can set the conditions to be determined as an end.  When specifying Inside, it is a condition that the robot enters into the specified Plane.  When specifying Outside, it is a condition that the robot is out of the specified Plane.

PlaneRelativeRobotLocal Property	For the end conditions related to positions, this property can set Local number that specifies the relative position or direction of Plane.

It is used in either of the following cases;

Relative is specified in PlaneType and Local is specified in PlaneRelativeOrg

Relative is specified in PlaneType and Local is specified in PlaneRelativeOrient.

Timeout Property	This property can set the time-out period of the object.  For one contacting motion, when the end conditions of force or positions are not satisfied, the program determines as failure of ContactProbe object even if exceeding the time specified by Timeout.  After the determination, the program ends the sequence according to AbortSeqOnFail or proceeds to the next force guide object.  When satisfying the end conditions of force, the robot moves to the next start position of the contacting motion and repeats the contacting motion.  When satisfying the end conditions of positions, the program determines that the hole is detected and proceeds to the next force guide object.
